headline: School Infrastructure Backlog Reaches RM4.5 Billion in 2026
context: The Public Works Department estimates RM4.5 billion is needed to address deferred maintenance across 10,300 public schools.

cards.big: RM4.5 billion maintenance backlog affects 10,300 schools across Malaysia in 2026
cards.sub: 3,200 schools report structural defects requiring immediate attention

cards.big: 47 school buildings condemned as unsafe since January 2025 forcing relocations
cards.sub: Temporary classrooms become permanent solutions lasting 5-10 years

cards.big: Annual school maintenance budget covers only 18% of identified repair needs
cards.sub: RM800 million allocated versus RM4.5 billion in documented requirements

cards.big: Sarawak longhouse schools face worst conditions with 82% needing roof repairs
cards.sub: Remote locations increase contractor costs by 200-300% compared to urban areas

cards.big: Children who learn in crumbling buildings receive a daily lesson in government neglect
Should school infrastructure be funded through dedicated bonds rather than annual budgets
